title: cicuta dichotoma
intro: Cicuta dichotoma, also known as tall water hemlock, is a highly toxic and poisonous plant native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, it is crucial to note that the plant contains a high concentration of cicutoxin, which can be fatal if ingested, even in small amounts. Despite the risks, some herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, have been made from the plant, but these should only be handled by trained professionals in a controlled environment.
